uefi_boot.UEFI boot process: UEFI firmware loads BOOTX64.EFI from EFI/BOOT/ on a FAT32 partition. Entry point is 'efi_main' or '#[entry]' in Rust. The firmware provides system table, boot services, and runtime services via handles.
uefi_boot.Setting up a UEFI project in Rust: Create a new project: cargo new --edition 2024. Add uefi = { version = "0.35", features = ["alloc", "global_allocator"] } to Cargo.toml. Use #![no_std] #![no_main]. #[entry] fn main() -> Status { ... }. Build with cargo build --target x86_64-unknown-uefi.
uefi_boot.UEFI memory map: Use boot::memory_map(MemoryType::LOADER_DATA). Iterate entries: entry.page_count * 4096 for size. MemoryType::CONVENTIONAL is free RAM. MemoryType::LOADER_DATA is reclaimable after use.
uefi_boot.UEFI protocols: Protocols are accessed via boot::open_protocol_exclusive::<T>(handle). Common protocols: GraphicsOutput (framebuffer), SimpleFileSystem (disk), LoadedImage (process loading), Pointer (mouse), SerialIo (serial port).
uefi_boot.UEFI file system: Use boot::get_image_file_system(boot::image_handle()) to get SimpleFileSystem. Open root with open_directory("/"). Open files with open_file(path, create, transient). Use File::read and File::write for I/O.
uefi_boot.UEFI GOP (Graphics Output Protocol): Get GOP handle: boot::get_handle_for_protocol::<GraphicsOutput>(). Open: boot::open_protocol_exclusive::<GraphicsOutput>(handle). Get mode: gop.current_mode_info(). Frame buffer: gop.frame_buffer().as_mut_ptr() as *mut u32. Pixel format: mode_info.pixel_format().
uefi_boot.Building a UEFI boot image: Compile to .efi: cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-uefi. Create FAT image: dd if=/dev/zero of=image.img bs=1M count=64, mkfs.vfat image.img, mmd -i image.img ::/EFI/BOOT, mcopy -i image.img boot.efi ::/EFI/BOOT/BOOTX64.EFI. Run in QEMU: qemu-system-x86_64 -bios OVMF.fd -drive format=raw,file=image.img.
uefi_boot.UEFI vs BIOS: UEFI is the modern replacement for legacy BIOS. UEFI boots in 32-bit or 64-bit protected/long mode. BIOS boots in 16-bit real mode. UEFI provides protocol-based driver model. BIOS uses interrupt-based 0x13 for disk I/O.

kernel_fundamentals.What a kernel does: A kernel manages hardware, memory, processes, and provides abstractions to user programs. Core responsibilities: memory management, process scheduling, interrupt handling, device drivers, IPC.
kernel_fundamentals.Monolithic vs Microkernel: Monolithic: all drivers in kernel space (Linux). Faster but less stable. Microkernel: minimal kernel, drivers as user-space services (Minix, QNX). More stable but slower due to IPC overhead.
kernel_fundamentals.Kernel entry point: The first code that runs after bootloader. Sets up stack, BSS, page tables, then calls kernel_main. Example: kernel::init() in kernel/mod.rs, called from main().
kernel_fundamentals.Panic handling: In no_std, define a #[panic_handler] function. Usually halts the CPU in a loop to prevent undefined behavior. Can print messages to UEFI stdout before halting.
kernel_fundamentals.Global static state: In kernel development, global state is common but unsafe. Wrap in UnsafeCell<T> and implement Sync manually. Use static mut or static with interior mutability. Example: pub static VFS: SafeVirtualFileSystem = SafeVirtualFileSystem::new();

memory_management.Page frames: Physical memory divided into 4KB pages. A page frame allocator tracks which frames are free/used. Simple implementation: bitmap or stack of free frame numbers.
memory_management.Page tables: x86_64 uses 4-level page tables: PML4 → PDPT → PD → PT → 4KB page. Each table has 512 entries. CR3 register holds the base address of PML4. Switching page tables = switching address space.
memory_management.Heap allocator: In no_std, implement alloc::alloc::GlobalAlloc trait. Methods: alloc(layout) and dealloc(ptr, layout). Simple bump allocator: increment a pointer. Better: buddy allocator or slab allocator.
memory_management.UEFI memory allocation: UEFI boot services provide allocate_pool() and free_pool(). After ExitBootServices, these are unavailable. Kernel must set up its own allocator before this point or use the uefi crate's global_allocator feature.
memory_management.Memory map from UEFI: See 'UEFI memory map'. Key for determining available RAM before setting up the kernel's own page frame allocator.

process_management.Process table: A fixed-size array of Process structs. Each entry has PID, state, registers, stack pointer, memory context. Simple implementation: [Option<Process>; N].
process_management.Process states: Ready (loaded, waiting for CPU), Running (currently executing), Sleeping (waiting for I/O/timer), Terminated (finished, waiting for cleanup).
process_management.Context switching: Save current CPU registers (general purpose, stack pointer, page table base) to current process struct. Load saved registers of next process. In x86_64: use swapgs, push/pop registers, then iretq or lret to jump to new process.
process_management.Scheduling: Round-robin is simplest: maintain a queue of ready processes, give each a time slice (e.g. 10ms). Timer interrupt triggers scheduler. More advanced: priority queues, multi-level feedback queue.
process_management.Process manager example: A process manager can use a fixed-size table of Process structs with name, PID, state. Spawn creates entry, kill marks as empty.

pci_devices.PCI bus scanning: Scan via port I/O: write to 0xCF8 (address), read from 0xCFC (data). Address format: bit 31 = enable, bits 23:16 = bus, 15:11 = slot, 10:8 = func, 7:0 = offset. Read vendor/device ID at offset 0. Class code at offset 0x08.
pci_devices.PCI driver matching: Match drivers by class code: 0x03 = VGA/graphics, 0x02 = network, 0x01 = mass storage, 0x04 = audio, 0x0C = USB. Subclass refines: 0x0106 = SATA, 0x0108 = NVMe. Vendor ID refines further: 0x8086 = Intel, 0x10DE = NVIDIA.
pci_devices.PCI implementation example: A PCI scanner can scan buses 0-7, slots 0-31, functions 0-7, storing up to 32 devices. Driver matching based on class/vendor ID.

filesystems.FAT32 structure: MBR → Reserved sectors → FAT (File Allocation Table, 2 copies) → Root directory → Data region. Cluster is unit of allocation (typically 4KB = 8 sectors). Directory entries are 32 bytes with name, size, attributes, cluster pointer.
filesystems.In-memory VFS: A Virtual File System sits between kernel and physical FS driver. Presents uniform interface: open, read, write, close, list. Routes to appropriate physical FS driver (FAT, Ext2, etc.). Can use in-memory Vec<File> for early development.
filesystems.VFS example: A VirtualFileSystem can store files as Vec<File> with name (String) and data (Vec<u8>), with a thread-safe wrapper for static access.
filesystems.Persistence strategy: UEFI SimpleFileSystem protocol can read/write real files on FAT partition. Call boot::get_image_file_system(boot::image_handle()) to get protocol. Opens files on the same partition the boot image is loaded from.

build_errors.cargo build fails: target not found: Run `rustup target add x86_64-unknown-uefi`. This adds the UEFI target to your Rust toolchain.
build_errors.linking with cc failed: no such file or: Install linker: sudo pacman -S lld (Arch) or use cross-compilation toolchain. Or add -C link-arg=--subsystem=efi_application to RUSTFLAGS.
build_errors.undefined reference to _Unwind_Resume: Add panic = "abort" to both [profile.dev] and [profile.release] in Cargo.toml.
build_errors.cannot find crate uefi: Add uefi = { version = "0.35", features = ["alloc", "global_allocator"] } to [dependencies] in Cargo.toml. Run cargo update then cargo build.
build_errors.error[E0463]: can't find crate for core: The target spec is missing. Run `rustup target add x86_64-unknown-uefi` or use the correct --target flag.
build_errors.error: #[panic_handler] not found: Add a panic handler function with #[panic_handler] attribute. Must have signature fn(info: &PanicInfo) -> !.
build_errors.error: no global memory allocator found: Use uefi features = ["alloc", "global_allocator"] or implement GlobalAlloc trait manually.
build_errors.error: using if let on an Option require: Update Rust: rustup update. Or restructure code to use match instead of if let.
build_errors.error: format argument must be a string: In no_std, write! macro requires the format string to be a literal. Use write!(out, "literal: {}", value).
build_errors.QEMU shows black screen: Make sure OVMF is installed: pacman -S edk2-ovmf. Use correct path: -bios /usr/share/edk2/x64/OVMF.4m.fd. Ensure the .efi file is correctly placed in the FAT image.
build_errors.System boots to shell but keyboard doesn: Check UEFI keyboard input: use uefi::proto::console::text::Key. Make sure input::init() is called. In QEMU, ensure -display gtk or -nographic is set correctly.
build_errors.Multiple definitions of _start: UEFI applications don't need a _start symbol. Use #![no_main] and let the uefi crate's entry point handle it.

package_system.Package format: A package struct can have name, version, description, category, source, entry. Source specifies where to fetch: Builtin, GitHub, or package registry.
package_system.Package database: A package database manages installed packages with methods: install, remove, find, list. Can include builtin packages from a hardcoded catalog.
package_system.App registry: An app registry tracks which applications are launchable. register() adds entry point, unregister() removes it, is_registered() checks before launching.
package_system.Catalog system: A catalog system can return categorized packages. Categories: system, dev, network, media, fun, etc. Provides search and filtering.
